Conditional guarantee is so you could offer, a conditional guarantee would be like, go through all the material, do all the homework,

## [36:21 - 39:25]

send me a copy of your homework that you did and if you don't get results, then I give your money back. So you only want to do a conditional guarantee if it's in addition to a normal unconditional guarantee. So it could be, hey, let's see, it would be like I offer 100%, I earn credit guarantee. Just let me know anytime within 30 days and you'll get your money back, no questions asked, no hard feelings. And to take it a step further, I give a full-out double guarantee. If anytime in the first year, after you've gone through the work, done the homework, if you're not getting the results you want, then just let me know and I'll give you your money back. And so there, what you've done is a conditional guarantee might be they have to show that they've put in the effort, they might have to show they didn't get the results and often that's a longer time period, but it's conditional that they have to show evidence of having done something. After we finish a launch, several months later, after we go through the whole period of the payment period, everything like that, after the fact, I'll get an aggregate number. This is the refund rate. And this is what the time frames at refunds came in. They spiked around the third week. They dropped off and they spiked up again on day 29. And this is the percentages. So I'll get those aggregate numbers after the fact, usually a couple months after the fact when I'm not terribly attached to them. But the best thing to do is outsource those refunds.

It's get someone else to handle them. It's just bad juju. You're in the middle of a seed launch. You're delivering it. You're having fun. And all of a sudden, you're about to go on a call. And it's just, it's not good energy to be seeing those coming into your inbox. You want to see the orders come into your inbox, but you don't want to see the refunds come into your inbox.
